data.Modification: Iodoacetamide derivative/Addition of Glycine/Addition of G
Loss of O2; nitro photochemical decomposition

project.title: rat, urine, LC-MS/MS -  Two predominant MUPs, OBP3 and MUP13, are male pheromones in rats
project.projectDescription: Two MUPs of rat urine, odorant-binding protein 3 (OBP3) and MUP13, were identified by LC-MS/MS,and found to be male-biased and androgen-dependent and then confirmed to be male pheromones in rats.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: We used the IEF method to separate the MUPs in male rat urine, and then selected the three most abundant bands to perform the In-gel digestion. After that, the extracted peptides were analysed by LC-MS/MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The raw MS data was analysed by the Proteome Discoverer software system (version 1.4.0.288; Thermo Scientific, USA) and searched against the Rattus norvegicus database (SwissProt, 2012). The matching proteins with a high peptide confidence for at least one unique peptide were considered significant, and the acrylamide modification of cysteines and methionine oxidation were set as fixed and dynamic modifications, respectively.

Comment[IonizationType]: EASY-Spray nano-electrospray ionization source (Thermo Scientific, USA)
Comment[Separation]: trap column (Acclaim PepMap100, 75 μm × 2 cm, nano Viper C18, 3 μm, 100 Å) and separated on an analytical column (Acclaim PepMap RSLC 75 μm × 15 cm, nano Viper C18, 2 μm, 100 Å) (both Thermo Fisher Scientific, USA)
Comment[GradientTime]: The linear gradient of buffer B increased from 0% to 30% in the first 60 min, to 95% in the next 10 min and was then sustained for 20 min at a flow rate of 300 nL/min.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: two missed trypsin cleavage sites were allowed.
Comment[NumberOfFractions]: each of the three predominant bands from an IEF gel of urine samples
